channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KREX 5

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during 5.2 quake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KTLA 5

item.title: WATCH: College football trophy comes apart in Vance's hands at White House
item.description: The College Football Playoff National Championship Trophy came apart during a White House visit by the Ohio State Buckeyes football team Monday as Vice President JD Vance tried to pick it up.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The great Easter basket question
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury selection to begin in Harvey Weinstein's retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The last time a New York City jury sat in judgment of Harvey Weinstein, the ex-movie studio boss was convicted of rape and sentenced to 23 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: A new study has found that fewer people crossed state lines for abortions in 2024 than the previous year.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Colorado deputy gets 3 years in prison for fatally shooting man who called for help
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of killing a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help was sentenced Monday to three years in prison, the maximum sentence, by a judge who said the shooting was about power.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Getting the IRS on the phone is more difficult this tax filing season, experts say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Taxpayers calling the IRS for help processing their taxes this filing season may find it harder than normal to get someone on the phone, experts say, a problem that is only expected to get worse next year with staffing cuts that could slash the workforce considerably.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security payments of up to $5.1K going out soon
item.description: This is all due to the Social Security Fairness Act, which was signed into law in January by former President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: Jeff Bezos takes a tumble while circling Blue Origin capsule carrying fiancée: Watch
item.description: Blue Origin founder Jeff Bezos was seen taking a fall to the ground nearly head first, after the New Shepard space capsule returned to Earth in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Diddy pleads not guilty to additional charges in new indictment
item.description: The music mogul faces federal charges of racketeering, sex trafficking and of transportation to engage in prostitution.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: A Palestinian activist expecting a US citizenship interview is arrested instead by ICE in Vermont
item.description: A Palestinian man who led protests against the war in Gaza as a student at Columbia University has been arrested at a Vermont immigration office where he expected to be interviewed about finalizing his U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle And Holly Ramer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rachel Morin murder: Jury finds illegal immigrant guilty of killing jogger
item.description: Maryland jurors on Monday convicted Victor Antonio Martinez-Hernandez, the Salvadoran migrant accused of raping and murdering Rachel Morin in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reported intruder arrested near UnitedHealthcare headquarters months after CEO's killing
item.description: Police in the Minneapolis suburb of Minnetonka said the suspect was arrested outside of the United Healthcare corporate campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Alleged member of Cuba's Ministry of the Interior arrested by ICE agents in Miami
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officials (ICE) arrested an alleged agent of Cuba’s Ministry of the Interior in Miami for unlawfully being in the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it is freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and contracts to Harvard University.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yellen sees 'very worrisome' pattern in Treasuries selloff
item.description: Former Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen has expressed concern over the economic uncertainty caused by President Trump's tariff policies, which she believes has led to a "really, very worrisome" trend for dollar-based assets.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames Zelensky for Ukraine war after '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: President Trump on Monday sought to distance himself from the war between Russia and Ukraine as his promise to end the conflict quickly has proven difficult to accomplish. "The War between Russia and Ukraine is Biden’s war, not mine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Meta faces historic antitrust trial that could force it to break off Instagram, WhatsApp
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. faces a historic antitrust trial beginning Monday that could force the tech giant to break off Instagram and WhatsApp, startups it bought more than a decade ago that have since grown into social media powerhouses.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY AP Technology Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beloved pet tortoise reunites with family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA says company whose sightseeing chopper crashed, killing 6, is ceasing operations immediately
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Federal Aviation Administration said Sunday that the helicopter tour company whose sightseeing chopper broke apart in flight and crashed in New York, killing the pilot and a family of five visitors from Spain, is shutting down operations immediately.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, WGN 9

item.title: A beloved pet tortoise is reunited with its family weeks after disappearing in a Mississippi tornado
item.description: KOKOMO, Miss. (AP) — Myrtle, a cherished pet tortoise, has been reunited with its family in Mississippi weeks after disappearing during a deadly tornado outbreak in March. “He’s been through a lot,” said Myrtle's grateful owner, Tiffany Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE BATES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wedding travel: How to minimize costs to make it work
item.description: Nearly 1 in 10 Americans are going to a wedding this summer that requires at least one night away from home
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, Fox 5

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Republicans fear Trump’s trade war could lead to political wipeout
item.description: Republican lawmakers say there’s a good chance that President Trump's trade war will boomerang on Republicans politically in 2026, as rising prices and shrinking growth could offset other accomplishments by the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man found with gunshot wound in the head arrested for DUI in Provo
item.description: A man who was arrested after a single-car crash on Friday tested positive for alcohol and drugs, and was found with an apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound to his head.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One of Deserae Turner's last wishes comes true as family, friends hold small horse show in her honor
item.description: On Saturday, April 12, family and friends made one of Deserae Turner's last wishes come true as she participated in a small horse show.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: These bars and restaurants are being called the most 'outstanding' in the country
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Foodies and restaurant lovers across the U.S. will need to add some new spots to their lists of places to eat with the James Beard Foundation officially announcing its finalists for this year’s most “outstanding” restaurant and bar in the country.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, ABC 4
